prompt: A photorealistic tailor's workshop, warm lamp lighting. A standard dress mannequin wearing a suit jacket that fits catastrophically wrong — the left shoulder is bunched up 3 inches too high, the right sleeve hangs 4 inches too long, the waist is cinched so tight it creates deep radiating stress wrinkles across the chest, the back collar sticks out awkwardly. The jacket was clearly custom-made for one very specific oddly-shaped human body and is completely unwearable on a standard form.
metaphor: A jacket custom-made for one body fits that person perfectly but looks broken on any standard mannequin, like a model memorizing training data.
